name: Illinois Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) — Curated Dataset 2010–2024
description: A curated, reproducible dataset derived from U.S. EPA Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) Basic Data Files for the state of Illinois, covering reporting years 2010 through 2024. The dataset integrates 15 annual CSV files into a unified resource with systematic quality assessment, curation flags, and multiple structured derivative outputs.

isBasedOn.name: Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) Basic Data Files

measurementTechnique: Annual self-reported regulatory data submitted by industrial facilities under EPCRA Section 313 using EPA Form R or Form A Certification Statement
variableMeasured: Total on-site and off-site chemical releases (pounds)
Off-site transfers for disposal, recycling, energy recovery, and treatment

distribution.name: CLEANED_RECORDS.csv
distribution.description: Flagged, cleaned record-level dataset. Unit: facility x chemical x year.

distribution.name: INDUSTRY_TIME_SERIES.csv
distribution.description: Annual aggregated totals by NAICS industry sector.

distribution.name: CHEMICAL_SUMMARY.csv
distribution.description: All-years chemical release totals ranked by total release volume.

distribution.name: CHEMICAL_YEAR_TIME_SERIES.csv
distribution.description: Release trends per chemical per year with chemical metadata.

curationActivities: Numeric prefix stripping from column names
Numeric type coercion for quantity fields
knownLimitations: PFAS data available from RY 2020 only
POTW calculation methodology changed in RY 2014
Releases are facility-estimated, not directly measured
Only above-threshold facilities report; does not capture all statewide emissions
Some chemical identities sanitised as trade secrets

fairAlignment.findable: Structured metadata, consistent naming conventions, hierarchical key-value outputs
fairAlignment.accessible: Open CSV formats, publicly downloadable source data, GitHub-hosted pipeline
fairAlignment.interoperable: Standardised EPA TRI schema, NAICS classification system, CAS numbers
fairAlignment.reusable: Full data dictionary, curation log, reproducible pipeline
